Title: Oxford Indoor Bouldering
Summary:
Hold on tight fellas (pun intended), OutdoorLads is now running a monthly indoor bouldering session in Oxford!
I'm sure many of us are no strangers to this kind of event, having become a very popular addition to ODL's catalogue with other chapters established in London, Bristol, Reading, Manchester and Sheffield.
For those new to the sport indoor bouldering is a form of rock climbing performed
without ropes or harness
on artificial walls (~3-4m) with crash mats underneath for safety. Here your technique and your problem solving ability counts just as much as your strength, so be prepared to work out both body and mind, all in a very social and friendly atmosphere (seriously, the puzzle solving nature of this sport acts as an amazing ice breaker when chatting to new people). I will also be there to welcome you into the group and answer any questions you may have, as well as offer climbing tips should you need them 😊.
We will be meeting at the
Gallery Bouldering
center located in the heart of Oxford, offering over 100m of bouldering wall (and growing) with a diverse range of routes catering for beginners all the way to Font 7C, so that there is something for all skill levels to enjoy. Afterwards we may also pop in for a pint at one of the many nearby pubs.
As with most climbing gyms there is a
waiver
that you will need to sign to check that you understand the risks involved and the proper climbing etiquette to follow to keep others and yourself safe while climbing. This can be filled out at reception but can also be done ahead of time by following the link
here
.
Please also note that this is a
liquid chalk only
gym, which can be readily purchased at reception.
Hope to see you there!
Difficulty & Distance: A range of climbs are available for all different skill levels to enjoy

Title: Gay City Bowlers Leeds - June
Summary:
OutdoorLads joins the UK's premier gay bowling night, Gay City Bowlers, for a fun social night in Leeds
It doesn't matter about skill level or experience - it's a fun inclusive night! We will meet at Roxy Lanes and will be allocated into lanes for two games.
There is a friendly competition across the night and the top 3 scorers of the night win medals. After the bowling we will gather to finish off the evening with a refreshing beverage.
More details about Gay City Bowlers, including details of events in other cities, can be found on their
Instagram
page.
IMPORTANT:
You must purchase your ticket for this event through the
Gay City Bowlers website
and then
sign up on this event page
! If you don't purchase a ticket prior to the event you will not be able to take part.
The tickets are £20 for two games. £1 is added by Gay City Bowlers to support their events across the UK, you can remove this or donate more.
Interested in keeping up-to-date with GCB news? Join their WhatsApp Community Group for Leeds:
https://chat.whatsapp.com/HfJVoLq7GhW6XqgEEfzJhU?mode=ems_copy_t
Image credits: Images supplied by OutdoorLads members, Gay City Bowlers and Pixaby Free Use - used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: All bowling abilities welcome.

Title: Aire Valley Circular Walk (Leeds)
Summary:
This is a steady
7.5‑mile (12km) circular walk
that offers a great mix of scenery and terrain.
We’ll set off from Rodley, making our way alongside stretches of the Leeds–Liverpool Canal and the River Aire, From there, the route gently climbs towards Rawdon, weaving through residential areas before heading into some lovely pockets of local woodland. Expect a combination of canal towpaths, riverside trails, and woodland tracks. Nothing too technical, but enough variety to keep it interesting.
We’ll pause partway through for a lunch stop, so please bring lunch / snacks with you.
If the weather is favourable, then we may finish off in a local pub for refreshment.
The walk is accessible by public transport (bus). Please see details in the Location section below.
Photos taken by the event leader with permission granted to Outdoorlads for further use. And by Kevin Waterhouse - Creative Commons licence -
Geograph
Difficulty & Distance: Mainly flat, you'll need to be capable of walking 7 - 8 miles.

Title: Midweek Meander - Iron Giant Meatballs
Summary:
Join Alex for a slow walk in Derbyshire, taking in the Iron Giant and ending up in the IKEA restaurant.
The Bennerley Viaduct, known informally as the Iron Giant, and occasionally described as
Derbyshire's Eiffel Tower
is a wrought iron structure that once carried a railway. It proved impossible to demolish, got listed in the 1970s and recently has been cared for by a friends group who have worked hard to get a decent footpath over the top of it.
IKEA
opened in Nottinghamshire in 1997
and revolutionised home furnishings in the East Midlands. Their low priced café is where we will finish our walk and while they are most famous for their meatballs, their menu includes veggie options. Signing up for an
IKEA Family card
in advance might give you a deal in the restaurant.
This walk includes a Nottinghamshire furniture giant and Derbyshire iron giant. The boundary between the two counties is the River Erewash which we will cross.
If this is your first ODL event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the ODL website.
Note:
If there are no places left on this event, join the waiting list, as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place.
Image Credits:
All images provided by the walk leader and permission is granted to ODL for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: This is largely flat with less than 100m of ascent, but some ramps up to the viaduct.

Title: Nottingham Inclosure Walk
Summary:
Join Alex for a slow and historic walk through Nottingham city centre.
This walk starts and finishes at two different tram stops with a stop at the end for a pint and a bag of crisps.
In the 1840s, Enclosure Acts up and down England were privatising common land and giving it to landowners. When this process came to Nottingham, the Corporation decided to ensure there would still be parks and green spaces in the city centre. The result is nearly six miles of connected paths with some surprising pockets of green in the very city centre. People have been using these walkways for over 150 years and this June, it's ODL's turn.
We will walk from the Trent to the Forest Recreation Ground. The footway is paved throughout, so the route is even accessible to motorised scooters should you need. This walk takes us through suburbs that were sparkly and new in mid-Victorian times, although some of them such as the
Meadows
and
St Ann's
have suffered decline in more recent times. This is a fully urban walk with no public toilets en route, apart from those in pubs and cafés on the way.
If this is your first ODL event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the ODL website.
Note:
If there are no places left on this event, join the waiting list, as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place.
Image Credits:
All images provided by the walk leader and permission is granted to ODL for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: This is a short, slow 9km walk on city paths, but there are some steeper bits.

Title: Woodhall Spa Day Walk
Summary:
Join Chris on a 9-mile (14.5 km) walk beginning in the village of Woodhall Spa, a place rich in history and character. Originally developed in the 19th century as a spa town after the discovery of a mineral spring, Woodhall Spa soon became a fashionable retreat. Its Edwardian charm is still evident today in the tree-lined avenues and elegant architecture, making it an inviting starting point for this scenic winter walk.
The route makes its way through the village before stopping at a local café for a short refreshment break. Recharged, we continue towards the much-loved Kinema in the Woods, a 1920s picture house nestled among the pines. Still in operation today, it is one of the UK’s oldest cinemas, famed for its nostalgic atmosphere and the live organ that entertains audiences during intervals. From here, the walk follows the Coronation Walk, a leafy avenue that links with the long-distance Viking Way and carries us further into the surrounding landscape.
Next comes the Woodhall Spa Sculpture Trail, tracing a former railway line which once connected the village with Horncastle. Along this stretch, keep an eye out for imaginative sculptures in wood, stone, and metal, each celebrating local wildlife and heritage.
The walk finishes with a tranquil loop through woodland and along bridleways before returning to Woodhall Spa. The route combines history, art, and nature to offer an enjoyable outdoor experience.
For those interested in a Parkrun before the event, the nearest one is at
Queen Elizabeth Grammar School
, just 3-miles (5 minutes) away.
Your First OutdoorLads Event?
We know that new members in particular like to know in advance more about what an OutdoorLads event will be like.
If this is your first OutdoorLads event, you should check out the information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.
Waitlist:
If this event is fully booked and you want to attend, just add yourself to the waitlist, because places inevitably become available closer to the date.
Image Credits:
All images taken by the event leader with permission for ODL use.
Difficulty & Distance: A 9-mile  (14.5km) walk. A 138ft (42 metre) Ascent.

Title: Board Game Cafe - Birmingham
Summary:
An evening of board games, food, drink and probably polite company.  Join Outdoorlads for an evening at the popular board game cafe in Birmingham.
There's a small cover charge of £2.50 per person, per hour. This is paid when the leader books the table, so expect a message the day before to confirm your attendnace.  We will likely play in small groups of 5.
If the event is full, join the waiting list and I'll book a additional tables if there is enough interest.

Title: Highland Coast Kayak and Wild Camping Expedition 2026
Summary:
Now a regular expedition in our Scottish calendar, join us and local guide/antarctic kayak expert
Will Copestake
on a 3 day guided, and catered, kayak tour of Scotland's beautiful northwest coast!
This year we’re developing a “North West Explorer” style trip. Which is essentially a 'wherever the weather and tides make sense’ package somewhere between Gairloch and the North Coast, within easy reach of Ullapool - for example we may do Kylesku to Oldany over three days (a fab trip), or Summer Isles, or south toward Rhuba Reigh - there is a large area to go at and the weather will make the final call.
Wherever we choose, we will venture from our start point to seek caves, archways and sandy beaches. We should see seals and possibly porpoise and dolphins. We will take the days at our own pace - you do not need to be an experienced kayaker or even a strong swimmer. As part of the itinerary, we will take regular pauses on wild beaches and offer the chance to brew a hot drink on a wood fired kelly kettle stove.
In the evenings, we will camp on the beach and enjoy a catered meal with a glass of wine - kayaks give us the chance to carry a little bit of luxury into the islands.
Itinerary
Friday 14th Aug - Breakfast at your own accommodation - 10am - meet guide at launch site (tbc), who will give out boats and equipment, get paddling, camp on beach with catered meal and wine.
Saturday 15th Aug - Breakfast at camp before more paddling, lunch on beach, paddle camp on beach with catered meal and wine.
Sunday 16th Aug - Breakfast at camp, paddle, lunch, paddle, return to shore for 3pm.
There is lots of accommodation in the general area and we will coordinate over the coming weeks. The event leader will be staying at a local campsite the night before the event (details below) and you will be welcome to join, but you will be responsible for making your own arrangements. There may be opportunity to add some days on to the end of the trip to explore the area further on foot so, if you are planning to extend your trip, do share plans with the group!
How to Book:
The cost of this fantastic expedition is
£465 for guided paddling, equipment, and food for three days
. You will need to book directly with the provider by emailing
will@kayaksummerisles.com
and quoting "Outdoorlads" - The guide's website is
here
. The OutdoorLads refund policy does not apply - terms and conditions are as per the activity provider.
Please be aware
that it is the paying of the event fee (or deposit if agreed by the provider) that secures your place on the trip, rather than signing onto the trip via the OutdoorLads site. Where deposits are accepted, balance payments will be requested by the provider prior to August 2026.
If a waitlist builds up, the provider may be able to Co-opt an additional operator and boats so it may be worth signing up!

Title: London Pride Parade & Float 2026
Summary:
Pride season is back – and this year, we’re celebrating in style. It’s our 20th Anniversary, and we’re bringing an OutdoorLads float to the party. 🚩
Pride in London is the biggest in the UK and one of the largest LGBT+ events in the world. With over a million people attending last year, it’s safe to say it’s kind of a big deal – and in 2026, OutdoorLads will be right in the heart of it all.
Whether you’re into hiking, hostelling, kayaking, cycling, camping, bouldering or climbing, this is your invitation to grab your red t-shirt and join us on the march. It’s your chance to show your pride, celebrate being part of OutdoorLads, and let London see how we #GetOutMore.
Marching at Pride is something special – the atmosphere, the crowds, the iconic London backdrop… it’s hard not to get swept up in it and in our anniversary year, it’s the perfect moment to showcase who we are, encourage new members, and celebrate everything OutdoorLads stands for.
So come and be part of it – bring your energy, your pride, and maybe your best dance moves🕺
Can’t make the parade? Come along and cheer us on from the sidelines, then join us afterwards for a drink and the celebrations.
New Members
Please feel free to join this as your first OutdoorLads event. If you have any questions then feel free to message me, or go to the "My First Event" section under "About Us".

Title: FRIDAY FELLS - High Cup Nick
Summary:
Join Friday Fells for an 8.5 mile circular mountain walk (575m ascent) in the North Pennines National Landscape - a great way to start your weekend early.
This walk takes in the spectacular geological feature of High Cup Nick and Murton Pike with spectacular views over the Eden Valley.
We'll start the walk from the village of Murton.  We cross over to High Cup Gill and follow the valley its narrow head.  It's a short scramble up to the rim, which we'll follow for a while, taking in the views all the while.  Eventually, we'll cross to Murton Pike (594m), a fantastic viewpoint.  From there, it's a short descent to the village and the car.
At the end of the day, we'll go for an optional drink at the
The Stag Inn
in Dufton.
You can find the planned route
here
, though we may vary this depending on the abilities and ambitions of the group and weather conditions.
Event full?
Please join the waiting list.  Places usually open up as the event gets nearer and, where necessary, we try to add additional leaders to increase the numbers, so you are highly likely to get a place.
New Members:
Please feel free to join this as your first OutdoorLads event, do note that this walk is likely to require some experience. If you have any questions then feel free to message me, or go to the "My First Event" section under "About Us".
Is it dog friendly?
Well-trained dogs are welcome but please note and abide by the
Countryside Code
. Please also remember not everyone loves dogs as much as you do so please be mindful of other members.

Title: Wednesday Shrewsbury Social (includes food option)
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for a chilled midweek night out in the Shropshire county town of Shrewsbury.
We will start and end at Shrewsbury railway station.  The last train east towards Birmingham is 22:40, and north towards Chester at 23:40, so like all good nights out (when over 30), you can be in bed with your cocoa before midnight. And it is a school night so that seems sensible!
There will be a gentle amble between pubs, including alongside the river, to help us also enjoy a hopefully sunny summer evening!
We will visit a handful of Shrewsbury pubs with an optional meal at the second pub - I’ll message out nearer the time for your preorders. (contact the leader for details if you are a last minute sign up.)
This is  an ideal event for new and newer members to make contact and catch up with the group and hear about what is happening locally.
Don’t worry if you can’t be there for 6pm or need to leave before half ten - that’s totally fine. Just let the leader know, we can share location with you and you can join when you are able.
Photo Credits: All photos taken by L Russell and permission is granted for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: It’s a night out, gentle stroll between pubs.

Title: FRIDAY FELLS - Bowscale & Bannerdale
Summary:
Join Friday Fells for an 8.5 mile circular mountain walk (725m ascent) in the Lake District's Northern Fells - a great way to start your weekend early.
This walk takes in the lower Wainwrights to the east of Blencathra and has spectacular views to the Pennines over the Eden Valley.
We'll start the walk from the village of Mungrisdale.  A short section on a quiet road will bring us to the hamlet of Bowscale. From there, we will begin our ascent, first along a track to the secretive Bowscale Tarn, from where we'll climb more steeply to Bowscale Fell (702m), our first Wainwright of the day.  From there we'll traverse across to Bannerdale Crags (683m), our second Wainwright.  A steep descent to a footbridge followed by a short climb, brings us to our third and final Wainwright of the day, Souther Fell (522m).  From there, it's a short descent to the village and the car.
At the end of the day, we'll go for an optional drink at the
Mill Inn
in Mungrisdale.
You can find the planned route
here
, though we may vary this depending on the abilities and ambitions of the group and weather conditions.
Event full?
Please join the waiting list.  Places usually open up as the event gets nearer and, where necessary, we try to add additional leaders to increase the numbers, so you are highly likely to get a place.

. Please also remember not everyone loves dogs as much as you do so please be mindful of other members.

Title: Fabulous Fan y Big
Summary:
Lose yourself here. Deep in the majesty of the Brecon Beacons in full summer.
The Bannau Brycheiniog National Park is magical at this time of year and our campsite on a working farm at the foot of Fan Y Big is right on the edge of the wilderness, with superb walking right from the tent flaps.
A camp fire under the stars, a fierce mountain day on Saturday,  meals together each evening; just some of the treats that await.
The farm's three-acre campsite has beautiful views towards the Brecon Beacons and the Black Mountains. The old stables have been converted into two spacious showers, a toilet block, washing up facilities and a snug room with a fridge/freezer and darts, radio and mobile phone charging facilities.
We want to make the weekend accessible and sociable for newer campers, so there will be a central area for cooking and eating with tables and some spare stoves if you don't have one.
As we are a smallish group, the singular Saturday activity will be a walk high in the main fans of the Beacons; this will be physically strenuous but not technical. On Sunday morning there will be a shorter walk option.
Campervans, caravans, motorhomes
: Sorry, but this site is suitable for tents only.
Dogs:
Unfortunately this event is not suitable for your furry friend.

Title: Disused Railway Walk to Loudon Hill
Summary:
Note the change of date to July 11th
An easy linear walk from the village of Darvel along a fantastic disused railway track to the Spirit of Scotland Monument
An easy yet super scenic route from the town of Darvel, meandering along a recently upgraded disused railway track that leads to the impressive Spirit of Scotland Monument beneath Loudoun Hill. Keep an eye out for the resident Highland Cows and remnants of the area's railway past, including the striking remains of a viaduct. Return via the same route to form a 7 mile walk
From the monument why not take the opportunity to make the short but steep ascent up Loudon Hill.  On a clear day your efforts will be rewarded with incredible panoramic views over Ayrshire, Lanarkshire, the Firth of Clyde and Isle of Arran.
Regular bus services to Darvel from Kilmarnock. No public transport to Spirit of Scotland monument. Parking available on or around
Hastings Square
, Darvel (KA17 0DR) or in the
public car park on Radoldcoup Road
(KA17 0JU).
Dogs:
The walk is suitable for dogs, which must be kept under control at all times, particularly near livestock.

Title: Glasgow evening walk - Carnbooth AA Battery
Summary:
Glasgow evening walk - Carnbooth AA Battery
An evening walk from Queens park to Linn park and Carnbooth AA Battery.
This former World War II artillery defense site was built to guard the industrial city of Glasgow. This was one of at least 43 similar sites around the city constructed to intercept any incoming air attack from German forces on industrial sites across the banks of the River Clyde.
A list of these sites and locations can be found here:
https://www.secretscotland.org.uk/index.php/Secrets/AABatteriesClyde
The battery site originally held four gun emplacements, two magazine buildings, a command post and a gun store. Interestingly, this site was later modified during the Cold War to counter Soviet threats. It was during this period that some of the buildings were demolished and a command post, engine house, and a perimeter security fence were constructed. The bolts from the metal structures of the anti-aircraft artillery still remain on the floor of each gun base - see photo.
This is primarily an evening walk through the greener parts of the south side. The ruins at the end are an interesting bonus.
Please don't expect The Guns of Navarone !

Title: Yeavering Hell!
Summary:
A 6.5 mile very hilly walk to Northumberland’s finest Hill Fort. This route takes in two peaks with outstanding views with a slow 3 mile walk up.
Yeavering Bell is probably the leader's favourite Northumberland inland walk but only if stop for a couple of breaks along the way. On the last venture out the leader saw a couple of adders but no wild mountain goats which was a shame.
Although nowhere near as high as The Cheviot it feels like it after the effort of the ascent. The route is a challenging walk and is rewarding with a lot to see.
Image credits:
Images provided by event leader and co-leader. OutdoorLads has permission to use them
Difficulty & Distance: Steep climbs first part of the walk. Rocky walk getting back.

Title: Glasgow Mural Trail
Summary:
An evening social walk around the artistic murals in Glasgow city.
These murals were intended to brighten up the gabel ends of tenements and are now a common sight around the city. They each have a story around them or commemorate a landmark event.
We won't get round every one, but will get in as many as possible over a couple of hours.
Background stories on each mural and rough locations can be found on the links below.
https://www.citycentremuraltrail.co.uk/murals/murallist
Use the link below during the walk to help you find the murals - not all of them are obvious!
https://www.citycentremuraltrail.co.uk/murals/muralmap

Title: FRIDAY Leisure Cycle - Anderton Boat Lift & River Weaver
Summary:
A leisurely social bike
ride
along lanes of the Cheshire Countryside, and a cafe stop at Whitegate Station.
Starting from near Knutsford, we follow lanes to Marbury Country Park, to our first stop at the Anderton Boat Lift, (unfortunately it is not working presently).
We then follow the River Weaver using Bridleways through Northwich to Whitegate and our lunch stop at the former station on the Whitegate Way (a Rail Trail). The return route follows the Whitegate Way and a Bridleway to cross back over the River Weaver, before lanes take us through the villages of Davenham, Plumley to Knutsford and depending on timing, maybe a possible extension to Tatton Park.
This event is around
30 miles
and is reasonably flat.  Whilst the majority of the route is on traffic free trails or quiet lanes, please note we do route along a few sections of busy roads to access the Boat Lift and will pass through Knutsford Town Centre on some busier roads.
We have plenty of time to complete the route and we will take it at a relaxed pace and no one will be left behind.
Road cyclists:
Please be aware some paths might be a little rough and bumpy for thin tyres, but doable none the less.  some sections of mud can be expected on the trails.
Mountain bikers:
Much of the route is on paved roads and expect to mix with traffic.
Pace and Terrain
This will be a leisurely cycle suitable for those of all levels of fitness. The majority of the route is on quiet country lanes, but we do have about 10 miles on the old railway line which can be muddy in places, but suitable for all bikes.
This event is open to cyclists of all abilities and bike types. There are a couple of climbs as we cross the Weaver Valley a couple of times.
If you're not a regular cyclist, please do make sure your bike is in good condition - check the tyres are well inflated, chain is oiled, and you've got a spare inner tube, drinks and snacks with you.
Photos:
All were taken by the Author.
Difficulty & Distance: Approximately 50km with 300m ascent

Title: Adur Evening Leisure Ride
Summary:
Join Nelly and Stuart on almost the longest day for a traffic free lesure ride up the downslink.
Setting off from shoreham we will head to the downslink, and old disused railway line. Cycling north with the river Adur besides us we will enjoy the views of the Shoreham airport and Lancing Chapel. Peddling deeper into the countryside we pass and abandoned cement works. Arriving at Bramber we will spend few minutes to visit the old ruins of the castle before we head back.
Once again on the downs link we will stop at a pub just outside of Shoreham for some food.
With a short ride back to our start point the event will end.
Please remember:
Helmet:
OutdoorLads requires you to wear one. No helmet no ride!
Punctures:
spare inner tube, it is a long push otherwise!
All images our the leader's own and used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: 11 mile ride on the flat.

Title: BIRMINGHAM Indoor Climbing - MONDAY - Experienced
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for an evening at Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre.
"EXPERIENCED" in this instance is those that have previously climbed & can answer YES to all three of these questions:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie in to a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly & confidently?
Please ensure you only sign up for this event if you
satisfy all three of the prerequisites as an experienced (independent) climber!
Never climbed before? Need a refresher on the basics? Answered NO to any of the three questions above? You will need to
attend one of the beginners (supervised) events
instead, where we will happily sign you in to be supervised (two people per leader maximum), show you the ropes & teach you everything you need to know to climb safely! Beginner (supervised) events are not offered every week.
If you are climbing with us for the first time, whether Experienced or Beginner, it is essential that you send a note to the Event Leader telling them about your previous climbing experience & your goals!
Bouldering
- Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer to do bouldering, you can sign yourself in & go bouldering (unsupervised) instead of, or as well as, roped climbing. Please meet the leader & the group at the start though; some others might wish to do bouldering too!
Please note that we will not teach lead climbing or lead belaying on this event -
we would suggest having some
lessons in Lead Climbing
by a qualified member of staff if this is something you're interested in pursuing!
If you are subsequently unable to attend, please cancel off the event as 'no-shows' are not only rude, but also frustrating for the leader & other attendees who may have travelled over an hour away to climb with you!
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing or would like a refresher, then this is just for you!! If you’ve climbed before, can safely tie into a harness & can competently belay, then there’s plenty of choices of top-rope & lead climbing on offer here.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.
You MUST register via this event page before coming to the centre!
To climb, you will need to:
Complete a
Registration Form
(please arrive early to do this in-person the first time) & pay a Registration Fee:
Annual Registration
is
£5
or
Day Registration
is
£3
Pay an
Entry Fee
directly to the climbing centre:
Standard Entry
is
£12.50
&
Bouldering Only
is
£8.50.
Hire
climbing shoes (
£3.50
) & a climbing harness (
£3.50
) if you don't own your own.
Please see Redpoint's
website
for current prices & note that prices are subject to change.
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
PLEASE NOTE:
All those attending the event must also be aware of the climbing centre's own terms & conditions of participation & abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre's staff.
Photos:
taken & used with permission of
R. Casabuena
&
K. Jones
.
Difficulty & Distance: This event is for EXPERIENCED (independent) climbers only!!!

Title: Kelham Pride (Sheffield) 2026 - The Parade!
Summary:
Join us as we celebrate and promote
OutdoorLads
at Kelham Pride, Sheffield!
🏳️‍🌈
The parade will set off from outside Kelham Island Museum and make its way through the heart of Kelham Island and Neepsend, finishing within the main Pride event area around Burton Road, where the wider celebrations will continue across participating venues and outdoor spaces.
Check out the
Kelham Pride Website
for more information about what will be happening on the day.
New to Outdoor lads or your first event? Come and join the celebrations - we would love to meet you!
The official start time of the parade is
1:00pm
outside Kelham Island Museum
👉 Please do
keep an eye out for messages from the event leader prior to the event.
Expect bold red tees, big energy, and plenty of pride as we march through the city together.
📍 The Plan
Meet from 12:30am
outside Kelham Island Museum
See the “Location” section below for full details.
We’ll be easy to spot, just look for the red OutdoorLads T-shirts (please wear yours if you have one).
As always, we want to showcase everything that makes OutdoorLads awesome, so if you're able, get involved by bringing gear that reflects your favourite activities:
Climbers
– Wear your harness. Bonus points for clipping on nuts, hexes or cams for a bit of jingly flair!
Cyclists / Mountain Bikers
– Ride along-side on your bike. Bells and horns encouraged for extra fun!
Walkers / Hikers
– Walking poles, rucksacks, hydration bladders (contents at your discretion!) and massive OS maps are all welcome. Bonus points if you act lost and ask the crowd for directions.
We’ll also have
flags to wave
, and heaps of enthusiasm to share!
Most importantly, don’t forget your red OutdoorLads T-shirt if you have one! if you're new to outdoor lads come as you are with pride.
🌈 After the Parade
We’ll be spending time around the main arena on Burton Road (the Peddler Warehouse area) after the parade, both to socialise and to represent OutdoorLads to potential new members.
We'll likely break into small groups to chat to the public, (ideally while enjoying a few drinks in the sunshine!).
📍 Details of where to meet after the parade will be announced on the day.
More details will be added here and communicated to participants as plans develop.
It’s going to be fantastic day.
We can’t wait to see you there!
🎉
Photos provided by OutdoorLads Members Jay and Russ, OutdoorL?ads has permission to use them. Main banner image - AI-generated.
Difficulty & Distance: Walking, waving and dancing isn’t too difficult :D

Title: Creating Event Pages - How not to balls it up.
Summary:
This is event is intended for new leaders or leader applicants who are due to be assessed soon. Any other leaders who feel that they need a refresher are also welcome to attend, subject to availability of places.
We will walk you through the process of creating your event page from scratch, including adding a venue and uploading your event images. We will also show you how to clone an event page and explain other important aspects of event admin such as pre-event messaging and completing the attendance register.

Title: River Tyne Route - Downstream to the Toon
Summary:
Join Steven for a pleasant easy linear valley walk hiking from a small village east and downstream to the quayside area of Newcastle Upon Tyne.
This is a much easier route, the paths are well maintained and signed and we will make a decent pace.
Along the way we will be visiting local landmarks, places of historic interest and nice venues for a swift half and a bite to eat.
Considering this route walks through the outskirts of the city this is a scenic and memorable route that largely coincides with the Hadrian's Wall Path and  River Tyne Trail
This walk is suitable for dogs, although they will be need to be on a lead in the small sections by the busy roads, a lot of the footpaths are enclosed away from farm animals. There may be a very small number of stiles to lift dogs across but as I recall they are mainly gates here.
This is an ideal way to start the weekend , there is a social later in the evening and this walk finishes in good time to check into a hotel to freshen up or return home and head back out.
https://www.outdoorlads.com/events/geordies-are-coming-toon-social-2607…
Image credits:
Images are provided by the event leader Steven and OutdoorLads has permission to use them.
Difficulty & Distance: 11.25 miles (18.1km) almost entirely flat

Title: River Tees evening stroll
Summary:
Join Steven and the OutdoorLads for an easy early evening wander along the riverbank visiting the pretty Georgian town of Yarm on the south bank of the Tees in the historic county of North Yorkshire.
The route takes in part of the Teesdale Way and wanders uphill to the village of Egglescliffe on the historic County Durham side.
This walk will be done at a slower relaxed pace with several stops along the way, a few points of historic note will be highlighted by the walk leader who lives locally and is very familiar with this part of the Tees Valley.
The gradients are easy and this is an ideal walk as an introduction to the group.  There are good wide reaching  views across the river and to a greater distant prospect over to the south towards the Cleveland Hills and further to the east to the Eston Hills above Middlesbrough.
So please come along to a shorter walk in pleasant surroundings along the River Tees a great way to spend a summer evening.
We are likely to gather in one of the many cafes or public houses in the area for a swift half ,tea or coffee after. If the weather is dry and warm there are outside areas and beer gardens.
Photo Credits: All photographs were taken by the walk leader Steven anf permission has been given for OutdoorLads to use them.
I
Difficulty & Distance: Easy introduction walk to the area 6.75km, 4.2 miles good paths

Title: A summer walk to Castle Eden Dene
Summary:
Join Steven for a hike in the little visited southeast corner of County Durham.
This route explores areas now managed as nature reserves and previously the land fell within the boundaries of Blackhall Colliery.
There are great views north and south along the cliff top section with only one steeper climb.
The half way point coincides with a good Public House so a longer stop here will give time for hot food.
The slow rise  from the Heritage Coast path into the magnesian limestone steep sided dene has many impressive tall trees towering above the footpaths and birdlife in abundance.
The trail passes under a high railway viaduct after leaving the coastal path this is a great place to photograph.
Although a little over 13 miles this route enables a quicker pace with a majority of good clear paths and minor roads.
Photo Credits: All photographs are taken by the leader Steven and permission has been granted for OutdoorLads to use.
Difficulty & Distance: The walk around 13 miles/21km. The terrain is not at all challenging and faster progress will be made.

Title: North Gloucestershire Social in Cheltenham
Summary:
An early evening, mid-week pub meet-up for members in this area. A chance for us to make closer links with other  members living nearby. An LGBTQ+ friendly venue.
Socials are an easy way to meet new and regular members and learn more about OutdoorLads before booking on your first walk, climb or hostel weekend. They're also great opportunity to find out what's coming up over the next few months with OutdoorLads in South Wales and the South West as well as nationally.
If you are coming along for the first time don't worry - the event leader will ensure that you are made welcome :-)
If you have any questions then please feel free to message the event leader. A food menue is attached if you think you may want to order food:
https://sandfordparkalehouse.co.uk/food-menu/
Want to Get Out More?
Why not check out the South Wales and South West Calendar
here
.
Photo Credits:
Photo's provided by Outdoorlads Members

Title: 5th Annual Nottingham Queer History Walk and Social
Summary:
5th ANNUAL NOTTINGHAM QUEER HISTORY WALK
Join Lucy and Shel, queer history guides, for a guided historical tour of Nottingham's LGBTQ+ history on a brand new walking route.
The guided walking tour of the city centre will explore our LGBTQ+ past and present with other added bits of history thrown in. Lucy and Shel plan stops along the way and end the walk with a buffet at Mojo around 17:00 pm.
EVERYONE IS WELCOME and they mean everyone! The walks have always been open to all and as long as you act respectfully to all. Under 18's must be accompanied by an adult. There will be some adult themes.
The organisers asks that we book a ticket via Eventbrite on this link
https://shorturl.at/wg4ao
This helps them track numbers for catering.
The event is completely
free to attend
with your buffet and quiz included! There will be donations tins raising funds for LGBT+ activities in Nottinghamshire.
The walk be around 3-4 hrs, through Nottingham's streets, and will be at a gentle pace with a few inclines.
Event organised by the Nottingham Forager and all photos have been given to us with permission to use them here.
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in the Midlands / ODL Central Region?
Sign up for
Central Region Events Notifications (Telegram Channel)
and the
Central Region Telegram Chat Group
, and bookmark our upcoming events list via the short link:
https://outdoorlads.events/central
.
Difficulty & Distance: Gentle meander through Nottingham City Centre

Title: Black Isle Coastal Walk with UK's third tallest lighthouse
Summary:
A nine mile circular walk with your OutdoorLads friends (even if you don't know them yet!)
Eight of the nine miles is coastal - hugging a narrow peninsula that juts out into the North Sea, between the Moray and Dornoch Firths. If the skies are clear, we will be rewarded with views of the hills and mountains of Caithness and Sutherland in the distance, crystal clear waters, an abundances of wildlife (seabirds, dolphins, whales (NB, the likelihood of seeing these declines the further down the list you get although whale bones are guaranteed!)), the third tallest lighthouse in the UK, some gorgeous sandy coves and rocky outcrops.
At the furthest point of our walk, with the lighthouse to our backs, we have a sea vista of some 340 degrees. We might want to dwell a while, unless there's a gale blowing!
So, bring your waterproofs and windproofs (just in case) some food, drink and enjoy OutdoorLads' company on a summer's day.
Dogs are welcome on this walk, under close control because of wildlife and livestock in fields.
Photos :
Taken by Rob Mitchell and used with permission.
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in Scotland?
Join our Telegram
chat
and
notification
groups and bookmark our upcoming events list via our
Scottish Calendar
.
Difficulty & Distance: 9 miles, 132m of ascent, coastal walk - exposed to the elements.

Title: A little bit of paradise
Summary:
Total distance about 14.5 km (approx. 9 miles) with 275m (900 feet) ascent. Approximately 5 hours, including lunch. This walk includes sections that are relatively flat (woodland), a gradual descent, and a fairly challenging ascent towards the end.
Starting from the Sneck Yatt car park, we descend towards the Paradise Trail, which follows part of the Cleveland Way. This initial section is through Boltby Woods. We arrive fairly quickly at High Paradise Farm. They offer camping and a tearoom that is open to the general public.
We then continue along the trail, adjacent to a moorland section, before entering a wooded section. There has been recent forestry activity, with many trees felled. However, once we cross this area, the panoramic views West across North Yorkshire more than justify the effort.
We gradually descend on a mix of narrow trails and broad open forestry "roads". We then pass Boltby reservoir, where I anticipate we will have lunch.
We continue our gradual descent until we reach the edge of Boltby village. This is where you see the ascent ahead of us. Don't be daunted. We can take as many pauses as we need.
On the day I visited, there were two paragliders up, making the most of the thermals and enjoying spectacular views. Although we will not be in the air, the view from the ridge is magnificent. It is then a relatively short walk (under a mile) back to the car park.
For those who may like a drink after, there are two options:
The Carpenters Arms
at Felixkirk and
The Dog & Gun
at Knayton.
Dogs: Well-behaved, under control dogs are welcome at this event.
Dogs should be kept on a lead whenever signposted, whenever we are in the vicinity of livestock, and at the event leader's request. Many stretches of this walk include sheep roaming. I would also note that there are a few up-and-over wall stiles towards the end of the walk, so you get a bit of an upper-body workout lifting your dogs over them. You'll need to make sure you're carrying sufficient extra water for your four-legged friend. We do not encounter much natural water on this walk. If you're unsure, contact the leader for guidance.
If the event is fully booked:
Please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
If this is your first OutdoorLads walk event,
we’ll make sure you feel welcome. Please
check out the My First Event page
, and don’t hesitate to drop me a message if you have questions or want to know more about this walk.
Image/photo credits:
All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use.
Difficulty & Distance: 9 miles, one steep ascent.

Title: Classic Pennine Moorland between Lancashire and Yorkshire
Summary:
A ramble of 7.5 miles (12km) with all the classic features of Pennine Moorland.
Our route follows cascading streams and moorland paths.
Among ubiquitous Victorian reservoirs there are vistas across grouse moors and outcrops of sandstone dating back 350 million years.
Curlews, Skylarks and Lapwings will  add to the magic of a moorland walk in summer.
If the weather has been inclement shortly before the walk a diversion is planned that will add approximately 2 miles to the walk.
If the weather is poor on the day, an alternative route of 6 miles will avoid the exposed moorland route.
This is a day walk on exposed moorland. Please ensure you are appropriately dressed for a day of walking in potentially very hot and sunny conditions or very wet and cold conditions.
Image / photo credits:  All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use and reuse.
Are you new to Outdoorlads and considering your first event?
Have a look at this link, hopefully it will help.
https://www.outdoorlads.com/my-first-event
Don’t worry if the event is full.
Do sign up and join the waiting list.
Often those on the main list cancel nearer the time.
There is a very high chance you will still get a place
Difficulty & Distance: 7.5 miles on moorland tracks and paths

Title: BIG Peaky Weekender 2026 (Hostel)
Summary:
🏔️ BIG Peaky Weekender: The BIG20 Edition 🏔️
Some events are just instant classics. Born during our 15th Anniversary celebrations, the
BIG Peaky Weekender
has quickly become a legendary staple of the OutdoorLads calendar. Now, as we hit our
BIG20
, we’re heading back to the heart of the Peak District for a celebration of where we’ve been and where we’re going!
⛺ Choose Your Base Camp
We have exclusive use of this fantastic outdoor centre. Whether you’re a hostel regular or a hardcore camper, we’ve got a spot for you:
Hostel
Camping
Teepee Camping Add-on
🥾 Getting Out More
This site is perfectly located for everything we love: walking, cycling, mountain biking, climbing, and paddlesports. Since it joined our calendar five years ago, it’s become a member favorite for its "ace in every way" location.
On Saturday, our Leaders will be out in force. Expect a "Greatest Hits" selection of hikes, rides, and local adventures. On Sunday there will be a small selection of events for before we head back home. We'll list the event pages below when we have them. If you're a Leader and keen to lead something, get in touch!
🍻 The BIG20 Social: Community & Connection
While other BIG events can be quite high energy, BIG Peaky stays true to its roots as a relaxed, social homecoming.
Our legendary bar-in-a-teepee is back for the BIG20! This is about getting out more and catching up with one another. No late-night disco—just a huge, welcoming space to chat, catch up with old friends, make new ones, and maybe get a bit competitive over some board games.
🍔 Fuelling the Anniversary
Look out for meal booking pages in the weeks before the event. You can pre-book Friday and Saturday evening meals, breakfasts, and packed lunches.
Hostel attendees have the option of catering for themselves in the hostel kitchen/dining facilities. However, you must tidy up everything after yourself as if you were never there. Like any other event, we do not have cleaning staff, and we rely on and expect attendees to clean up after themselves.
✨ Be Part of the Story
From its birth at our 15th anniversary to this massive 20th celebration, the BIG Peaky Weekender is all about the people. Whether you’ve been with us for twenty years or twenty minutes, you are part of the legend.
Don’t miss out on this chapter of our BIG20 year. Grab your spot now!
Dogs:
This event is not suitable for dogs - they are not allowed on site.
Campervans, caravans, motorhomes:
Due to the venues restrictions, we are unable to accept campervans, caravans and motorhomes.
Images:
Aerial shots kindly supplied by and used with permission from Will Allaway. Other photos taken by and used with permission from OutdoorLads Members and photographer William Hickey.
Difficulty & Distance: As much or a little as you please!

Summary:
🏔️ BIG Peaky Weekender: The BIG20 Edition 🏔️

The teepees can fit up to 3 people comfortably, 4 at a push. To reserve a teepee, book on to the camping event page, and then book on to this event page. If you're sharing, only one person in your party needs to book on to this event page, but they must all be booked on the camping page.
You don't need to tell us until you arrive who is staying with you in your teepee - tell us on arrival at registration. You can of course choose to have the teepee just for yourself!

Title: Lighthouse to Lighthouse 2026 South Tyneside
Summary:
This is an eight mile route that starts at a lighthouse at the mouth of the Tyne and takes in a very cool pub in a cave with great views.
Continuing southward along the rocky coastline by smuggler coves to end at a lighthouse in a coastal national park with another stunning view of what can only be described as Jurassic.
This is a mainly linear route but the views both ways are very different.
Photo Credits: All photographs were taken by the leader Steve and permission has been given for OutdoorLads to use
Difficulty & Distance: It’s a fairly flat walk with a few hills and it’s mainly on the sand.

Title: Wind In The Willows River Summer 😎 Sunday Saunter
Summary:
Join us for a relaxed and sociable 10-mile circular walk through some of the most quintessentially English stretches of the Thames Valley – with one cheeky hill to earn your lunch. This is classic
“Wind in the Willows” country
. The Wind in the Willows was inspired by this very stretch of river, where Kenneth Grahame spent part of his childhood in Cookham. As we walk beside the Thames, it’s not hard to imagine Ratty and Mole “messing about in boats” along these same waters. Think lazy riverbanks, boats drifting by, and just enough charm to make you feel like you should be carrying a picnic hamper (or at least a decent snack).
The route is 10 miles, however there is the option just to do the morning section (just over 5 miles) and take the train back from Marlow Station.
The Route:
Starting at Cookham Station, we will initially walk into the older centre of the village, past the Stanley Spencer Gallery. We will then walk through the churchyard of the scenic Holy Trinity Church and then join the Thames Path, following the river for about 1.5 miles.
Later we will diverge from the Thames Path walking uphill towards Winter Hill where we are rewarded with sweeping views across the Thames Valley. We will follow the ridge for some time before climbing back down and then taking a flatter route towards Marlow. Entering Marlow, we will walk over it's historic bridge (see more details below) before getting to it's lively High Street.
At lunch we will take an extended break enabling participants to do their own thing, whether grabbing a coffee and sandwich in one of the cafes or a picnic lunch in the riverside park.
After lunch we will re-join the Thames path, which is now on the North side of the river, and walk along it for 3 miles towards Bourne End with it's marina. At the railway bridge we cross back to the South side of the river, re-trace some of our steps from the morning walk and then take a path back towards Cookham Station following the railway line across fields and later passing by a golf course.
Depending on the time that we arrive back, mindful that for those taking the train services are just hourly, there may be time for a quick drink or tea and cake at the nearby cafe (which closes at 4pm on Sundays).
The Sites:
Cookham:
is a quintessential Thames-side village, widely regarded as one of the most picturesque in England. The village has a strong artistic identity, most notably through its association with Stanley Spencer, whose work vividly depicted local life; and his legacy is preserved in the Stanley Spencer Gallery.
Winter Hill:
located just above Cookham, is characterised by its elevated position and sweeping views across the Thames Valley and the Chilterns. Much if it is protected by the National trust.
Marlow:
is a lively and attractive Georgian town that blends historic character with a strong leisure and dining scene. The town’s elegant suspension bridge, designed by William Tierney Clark, is a notable landmark and the famous Chain Bridge in Budapest, designed by the same architect, is a larger scale replica of Marlow Bridge.
Bourne End:
is a village where the Thames meets the River Wye and has a marina and railway bridge which we will pass and cross. The area also played a role in early British film production at Riverside Studios.
The route can be found
here
:
Dogs:
Well-behaved dogs are welcome, the sections along the Thames Path can get busy, the lunch spot in Marlow can get very busy and there are sections walking along pavements alongside busy roads. Please abide by the Countryside Code (
https://tinyurl.com/rlqvp3h
) and remember not everyone loves dogs as much as you do so be mindful of other members.
Photos:
All photos the leader's own, with permission given to OutdoorLads for use.
Difficulty & Distance: 10 miles, relaxed moderate pace, mainly flat with just one hilly section around Winter Hill, total ascent 110 meters.

Title: Board Game Cafe - Birmingham
Summary:
An evening of board games, food, drink and probably polite company.  Join Outdoorlads for an evening at the popular board game cafe in Birmingham.
There's a small cover charge of £2.50 per person, per hour. This is paid when the leader books the table, so expect a message the day before to confirm your attendnace.  We will likely play in small groups of 5.
If the event is full, join the waiting list and I'll book a additional tables if there is enough interest.
Social events are great way to forge new friendships and talk about your experiences on OutdoorLads events, and to find out what is going on in the coming months.
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in the Midlands / ODL Central Region?   Sign up for
Central Region Events Notifications (Telegram Channel)
and the
Central Region Telegram Chat Group
, and bookmark our upcoming events list via the short link:
https://outdoorlads.events/central
.
Photos by Outdoorwill and used with his permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Social

Title: Nottingham Bouldering (Lenton) - MONDAY - Beginners Welcome
Summary:
Swap trainers for climbing shoes and join OutdoorLads for an evening of INDOOR BOULDERING at Nottingham’s newest bouldering venue
NOTE: If you are climbing with us for the first time
, either as an experienced climber or beginner, please send a note to the
Event Leader
telling them about your experience and goals. There is a limit to the number of beginners that we can accommodate at each session. Each beginner needs to be supervised so we need to ensure there are enough experienced climbers to help you out. Climbing is great fun! It's a fab workout, in a social environment, and anyone can do it! Give it a go!
What is Bouldering?
It's a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems' tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes it an excellent activity for newcomers to the sport. No wonder it's often regarded as the 'purest' form of climbing.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
I'm new to Bouldering - can I come along?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
I've been Bouldering before?
As above, everyone attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you are responsible for your own safety, actions and involvement at all times. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
To climb at Nottingham Climbing Centre you will need to:
-     Complete a climbing waiver for the centre (please arrive early to do this the first time, or better still complete it online before you arrive.) You can find the
link
to the waiver and bouldering safety video
here
on Nottingham Climbing Centre's website.
-     Pay the Lifetime Registration Fee - £6.00
-     Pay an Entry Fee directly to the climbing centre - £9.50 (ensure you mention ODL)
-     Hire shoes (if you don’t have your own) - £3.50*
*Equipment hire charges will be waived for your first visit to the centre!
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
IMPORTANT!
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Photos:
All images supplied by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: NOTTINGHAM Indoor Climbing (Basford) – Beginners welcome
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads at Nottingham Climbing Centre - (Basford)
Climbing evenings are held regularly and we tend to go to the local pub – The Lion for refreshments and a social gathering afterwards where they have a wide variety of beverages both alcoholic and non-alcoholic.
NOTE: If you are climbing with us for the first time
, please send a note to the
Event Leader
telling them about your experience and goals.
Nottingham Climbing Centre - (Basford)
Nottingham Climbing Centre is equipped with walls and routes to suit all skill levels and groups of climbers, whether you are a novice looking to try climbing for the first time, or an experienced lead climber, you’ll find an area to challenge you. With 28 Top Ropes routes available as well as 24 Lines of Lead climbing for the more advanced individuals. In addition, 6 auto-belays are available, so there is always the opportunity to climb, even if the numbers don’t match up. The available bouldering walls also provide that extra physical and mental challenge for those looking for something slightly different from rope work. Both Bouldering and Rope Climbing routes range from 4a – 8a.
Do I need to be Supervised as a Climber?
If you answer
NO
to any of the following three questions, then you will need to arrange to be supervised and will need to book onto a future beginners event.  If you can answer YES to all these questions, you can sign up to this event as normal:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie yourself to the end of a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly and confidently?
To come along, you will need to register via this event page and to climb you will need to pay an entry fee directly to the climbing centre when you arrive, please see their
website
for up-to-date prices. We ask that all members sign up through the event page to make sure the event leader has all information of the members attending.
To climb at Nottingham Climbing Centre you will need to:
-     Complete a climbing waiver for the centre (please arrive early to do this the first time, or better still complete it online before you arrive)
-     Pay the Lifetime Registration Fee - £6.00
-     Pay an Entry Fee directly to the climbing centre - £9.50 (ensure you mention ODL)
-     Hire shoes (if you don’t have your own) - £3.50*
-     Hire a harness (again, if you don’t have your own) - £3.50*
-     Hire shoes and harness - £6.00*
*Equipment hire charges will be waived for your first visit to the centre!
Can I just come along and boulder?
Absolutely! Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer bouldering, you can sign yourself in and do it, as long as you can fill in the climbing centre's form correctly, watch their
safety video
and can answer the simple safety questions at reception on your first visit. Meet the group at the start; some others might be bouldering. We will make sure we come and find you when we have our tea break!
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
Photos:
All images supplied by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Beginners welcome.

Title: BIRMINGHAM Indoor Climbing - MONDAY - Beginners
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for an evening at Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre.
*** Beginner (supervised) events are not offered every week ***
If you have never climbed before (or need a refresher on the basics), then this BEGINNERS EVENT is ideal for you! We will happily show you the ropes & teach you everything you need to know to climb safely!
Event pages are split into
BEGINNER & EXPERIENCED.
Please ensure you sign up to the correct event as we can only supervise two beginners at once per leader.
This event is for beginner climbers (those needing to be supervised) only!
If you are competent with how to tie in & belay (safely & confidently), then you will need to
sign up to the accompanying experienced (independent climber) event
instead.
If you are climbing with us for the first time, whether Experienced or Beginner, it is essential that you send a note to the Event Leader telling them about your previous climbing experience & your goals!
Bouldering
- Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer to do bouldering, you can sign yourself in & go bouldering (unsupervised) instead of, or as well as, roped climbing. Please meet the leader & the group at the start though; some others might wish to do bouldering too!
Please note that we will not teach lead climbing or lead belaying on this event -
we would suggest having some
lessons in Lead Climbing
by a qualified member of staff if this is something you're interested in pursuing!
If you are subsequently unable to attend, please cancel off the event. Spaces for beginners are limited & 'no-shows' are not only rude, but also frustrating for the leader & other attendees who may have travelled over an hour away to climb with you!
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing (or would like a refresher), there are plenty of top-rope routes to choose from after learning the essential climbing & safety techniques with one of our friendly, experienced climbing leaders.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.
You MUST register via this event page before coming to the centre!
To climb, you will need to:
Complete a
Registration Form
(please arrive early to do this in-person the first time) & pay a Registration Fee:
Annual Registration
is
£5
or
Day Registration
is
£3
Pay an
Entry Fee
directly to the climbing centre:
Standard Entry
is
£12.50
&
Bouldering Only
is
£8.50.
Hire
climbing shoes (
£3.50
) & a climbing harness (
£3.50
) if you don't own your own.
Please see Redpoint's
website
for current prices & note that prices are subject to change.
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
PLEASE NOTE:
All those attending the event must also be aware of the climbing centre's own terms & conditions of participation & abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre's staff.

taken & used with permission of
R. Casabuena
&

.

Title: Ashgill Force and Tynehead Circular
Summary:
A relaxed circular
13km
walk in the North Pennines taking in the dramatic Ashgill Force waterfall.
Including the upper South Tyne valley, open moorland scenery and quiet countryside around Tynehead.
This is a friendly social walk with plenty of opportunities for photos, chats, and breaks.
Expect waterfalls, riverside paths, old mining landscapes, and wide Pennine views.
Image / photo credits:  All leader's own images - with permission for OutdoorLads to use and reuse.
Are you new to Outdoorlads and considering your first event?
Have a look at this link, hopefully it will help.
https://www.outdoorlads.com/my-first-event
Don’t worry if the event is full.
Do sign up and join the waiting list.
Often those on the main list cancel nearer the time.
There is a very high chance you will still get a place
Difficulty & Distance: There are some hills which can be steep and the moorland can be difficult to walk on.

Title: Northern Ireland's Causeway Coast
Summary:
We're off over the Irish Sea for four nights to explore the Causeway Coast.
Our hostel has a sea view and is in an ideal location for seeing the various attractions of the area - top of the list is the Causeway Coast itself with its hexagonal basalt columns, this being part of a fine coastal walk. Also within walking distance of the hostel is Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge, perhaps not for those with a fear of heights as it hangs 30m above the sea!
A short drive away is Ballycastle with the historic Bonamargy Friary and it's harbour, this being the port for the short sailing to Rathlin Island - an island six miles long with tranquil walking trails and views back across the causeway coast. If we go a little further east we find ourselves in the Glens of Antrim, an area of ancient woodlands and waterfalls with several beautiful walking trails.
There is lots more to see and do, but why not just come along and discover this stunning part of Northern Ireland....
Our hostel has lots of social space, free table tennis & pool and clean, comfortable dorm rooms - plus a pub 100m away. It's absolutely perfect as a base to explore the local area and this stunning coastline.
For some travel guidance, do check the information provided below the location map.
Dogs:
This venue isn't suitable for dogs.
Photos:
by AMD and Liam Russell, used by OutdoorLads with full permission.

Title: Ross On Wye Pride
Summary:
Come join us as Ross on Wye celebrates Pride 2026 so, whether you like climbing, bouldering, hosteling, cycling, camping, canoeing or day walks grab your OutdoorLads RED T-shirt and come join in the fun of Pride and help show Ross on Wye that OutdoorLads does #GetOutMore!
The event is to be hosted by the Hope and Anchor, there will be a March around town starting from the bandstand and finishing at the Hope and Anchor. Food and drink will be available at the venue, inside the main pub, in the Pavilion and also outside from the hut. There will be various live entertainment acts throughout the afternoon with a range of community activities.
PRIDE STAND :
OutdoorLads will be having a stand at Ross Pride and it would be great if you wanted to spend a couple of hours to help man the stand
Photos:
Photos from Outdoorlads & Oxford Pride and used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Helping encourage new members to join!

Title: Dales of the White Peak
Summary:
This walk highlights the classic dale features of the White Peak, itself part of the wider Peak District National Park.
Starting in the pretty market town of Bakewell, we head upwards onto Haddon Fields and the glorious 360 degree vistas of the White Peak including the villages of Stanton in the Peak and Youlgreave.
We head down to the historic hamlet of Alport and into our first dale - Bradford Dale. The river Bradford runs through the dale and there are signs throughout of industrial heritage. The dale itself is lush with greenery and has a small intimate feel.
Finally, we make our way up and out of the dale and head over on the Limestone Way to a very different Dale- Lathkill Dale. This large, expansive dale is popular with walkers with foreboding white cliffs and the River Lathkill running its way through.
Toward the end of our walk we head sharply towards the small village of Over Haddon before heading back down to Bakewell.
Difficulty & Distance: This walk is approximately 19km with some sharp ascents amongst the rolling landscape. Total ascent 400m

Title: London Ten Pin Bowling with Optional Pub Social 🎳😃
Summary:
GET SOCIAL WITH OUTDOORLADS! Great for new and regular members!
If the event is full, please

, and we will amend our booking with Rowan's if availability permits.
Ten pin bowling and optional pub social which will now take place on a regular monthly basis, as the 1st event was so well received by the attendees. All are welcome on this event, whether you’ve bowled before or you’re a complete beginner. If you’ve never bowled before come along and have some Fun! I’m happy to show/demonstrate how I bowl. Pretty average!?
Come join me and the other guys for an evening of ten pin bowling fun in north London.  We have a group booking for 19.00 Wednesday night at Rowan's Ten Pin Bowl in Finsbury Park. All Welcome! Please arrive and check in by 18.45 so we have time to get all set up! We have two games per person booked.
PLEASE NOTE:- There is an admission fee to Rowan’s (Not included in the event charge). This is payable upon entry.

THE T&C’S OF ROWAN’S ALSO REQUIRE PHOTO ID ON FRIDAY & SATURDAY EVENINGS. (Obviously probably dependent on what age you appear to be).
You DO NOT need to book with Rowan's. I will make the booking prior to event once appropriate attendee numbers are known.
The cost is £21.60 (Fridays) per player for 2 games of ten pin bowling.
Difficulty & Distance: Bowling - very easy!

Title: Oasts and Ghosts in the Low Weald of Kent
Summary:
An introduction to the
Low Weald of Kent
, riding alongside the hop farms, oast-houses, woods and wineries that give this area its distinctive style, coupled with a climb up the Green Sands Ridge for views over the High Weald and North Downs.
We may catch the end of the bluebell season; they have put in an early appearance this year, but look stunning in the dappled shade of the Kent woodland.
This event is aimed at
experienced riders
with a reasonable fitness level who can ride at an
average pace of 14mph.
If this is faster than you would normally ride, or if you've not been in the saddle for a while, then please consider attending a Leisure Ride or an easy road ride in the first instance. This ride is
70km
, so again please ensure you are able to complete this distance comfortably.
Route Overview
We will start in the village of Marden, and head out to the Kent Weald countryside. Keep an eye out for the distinctive oast houses, many of which have been converted into homes. These round or square building were used in the drying of the hops to make beer.
After a short ride beside farmland, a hop field and ancient woodland we will pass Hush Heath Winery. The grapes here are grown on the same chalk ridges that extend to the Champagne Region of France.
We then continue east, skirting the village of Staplehurst, through the village of Frittenden and cross country to Bethersden.  Watch out for grass and grit in the centre of these narrow lanes and also be on the look out for tractors and people riding horses. There is a shop in Bethersden to get supplies if needed.
We can stop for lunch in Bethersden at The Bull Inn (
https://www.bullinnbethersden.co.uk/dining
) who do a range of baguettes and other pub snacks. Or we can continue and stop in Pluckley.
When we leave the village we head north on the slow steady incline to Pluckley. Pluckley sits on the Green Sands Ridge, that runs from Sevenoaks to the coast. This is rumoured to be the most haunted village in England, so keep an eye out for any ghosts!
The alternative lunch stop is The Black Horse in Pluckley (
https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/#menu
) also with baguettes and pub bites.
We then ride along the Green Sands Ridge, you will see to the High Weald on the south side, and to the north the North Downs. When we get to the village of Egerton, we come down from the ridge and start to wind our way towards Headcorn.
We skirt the edge of Headcorn and make our way back to finish in Marden.
Weather Conditions
If unfavourable weather is forecast, I will postpone the ride. I’ll notify participants as early as possible.
Please check messages before leaving for the event.

Title: Rescheduled Evening River Walk - The Tees towards sunset
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads on a midsummer evening for an almost entirely flat easier 14.5km/ 9 mile walk in County Durham.
Walking on the edge of the railway town of Darlington an ideal opportunity to meet local members and as an introduction to our group.
We will walk into the evening sunshine and see both the River Skerne and the River Tees at close quarters.
This walk is suitable for dogs and is indeed a popular dog walk for the locals.
Photo Credits;: Images used here are taken by the leader Steven and permission is granted for their use
Difficulty & Distance: 14.5km 9 miles almost entirely flat
